resposta:
solution: x = x ^ y; return x.length() - 1;
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We use bitwise XOR operation (^) to calculate the Hamming distance by finding the number of bits that are different between x and y. Since XOR operation has constant time complexity, the time complexity is O(1). The solution only uses a constant amount of space to store the result, so the space complexity is also O(1). The energy consumption is low as the operation is performed in constant time.
